title: When He Should Laugh by Edward Thomas — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
WHEN he should laugh the wise man knows full well:
For he knows what is truly laughable.
But wiser is the man who laughs also,
Or holds his laughter,

title: But These Things Also by Edward Thomas — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
BUT these things also are Spring's--
On banks by the roadside the grass
Long-dead that is greyer now
Than all the Winter it was;

The shell of a littl

title: Will You Come? by Edward Thomas — Love Poetry Lines
content:
WILL you come?
Will you come?
Will you ride
So late
At my side?
O, will you come?

Will you come?
Will you come
If the night
Has a moon,
Full and brig

title: In Memoriam (Easter, 1915) by Edward Thomas — Love Poetry Lines
content:

This Eastertide call into mind the men,
Now far from home, who, with their sweethearts, should

title: Fifty Faggots by Edward Thomas — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
THERE they stand, on their ends, the fifty faggots
That once were underwood of hazel and ash
In Jenny Pinks's Copse. Now, by the hedge

title: The Cherry Trees by Edward Thomas — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
THE cherry trees bend over and are shedding
On the old road where all that passed are dead,
Their petals, strewing the grass as for a wedding
This ear

title: Some Eyes Condemn by Edward Thomas — Love Poetry Lines
content:
SOME eyes condemn the earth they gaze upon:
Some wait patiently till they know far more
Than earth can tell them: some laugh at the whole
As folly of

title: Melancholy by Edward Thomas — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
THE rain and wind, the rain and wind, raved endlessly.
On me the Summer storm, and fever, and melancholy
Wrought magic, so that if I feared the solitu

title: It Was upon by Edward Thomas — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
IT was upon a July evening.
At a stile I stood, looking along a path
Over the country by a second Spring
Drenched perfect green again. "The lattermath

title: Tall Nettles by Edward Thomas — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
TALL nettles cover up, as they have done
These many springs, the rusty harrow, the plough
